text: We will be presenting a case of robotic-assisted resection of an intragastric tumor. Thank you for the opportunity to present our work. This is a fifty-seven-year-old female who presented after a preoperative EGD performed for opaline weight loss, which found a gastric mass at the cardia. She subsequently had EUS and biopsy, which was concerning for a five-by-three leiomyoma near the gastroesophageal junction. She was recommended for a total gastrectomy due to inability of endoscopic resection and she came to us for a second opinion.

The robotic ports are placed, including four robotic ports and an air seal port located 15 cm sub-xiphoid. A Genzyme retractor retracts the liver through the air seal port. Three ports highlighted in red are converted to intragastric ports accessed using an 11 mm Versastep. The stomach is insufflated endoscopically to 15 mmHg, and the abdomen is desufflated to juxtapose the stomach to the abdominal wall. Under endoscopic visualization, radially dilating Versastep ports enter the stomach, enhancing traction for the robotic ports placed over them. The stomach remains insufflated to 15 mmHg for the procedure.

The surgeon uses fenestrated Maryland forceps and hot shears to perform a complete submucosal resection of the lobulated mass at the gastroesophageal junction. The dissection is carried out circumferentially in the submucosal plane, with cigarettes used for retraction and blotting. Hot shears are employed to maintain hemostasis, while the robotic arms facilitate precise dissection above the gastric muscles.
The surgeon uses bipolar or fenestrated Maryland forceps to cauterize areas of bleeding encountered during the dissection.

The surgeon uses a five-millimeter endocatch bag, introduced through the robotic ports, to remove the specimen from the submucosa. An endoscope with a snare is used to grasp the end of the endocatch bag, facilitating the transoral removal of the specimen bag.

The surgeon closes the mucosal defect in two layers using a 3-0 Maxon V-lock suture, tying the ends together. An endoscope is inserted to confirm no narrowing at the gastroesophageal junction. The robotic arms are removed from the intragastric ports under direct endoscopic visualization. The stomach is desufflated, the abdomen is insufflated, and the intragastric ports are closed with interrupted 3-0 silk sutures.

A leak test was performed by insufflating air endoscopically through the stomach. Patient had an uneventful recovery. She had a gastrographic swallow on post-op day one, which was negative for any leaks. Her diet was advanced, and she was discharged home on post-op day two on a full liquid diet. She had minimal pain and required no narcotics. We maintained her on a PPI to decrease gastric secretions. Our final path was consistent with a leiomyoma. Thank you so much for your attention.
